skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos through the RunComfy CLI. Routes requests across video models including HappyHorse 1.0, Wan-AI Wan 2-7, ByteDance Seedance, Kling, Google Veo, MiniMax Hailuo, and Dreamina. Supports text-to-video, image-to-video, and video extension. Picks the right model based on user intent such as quality, speed, cost, audio sync, or multi-shot consistency.
skill.summary: Generate AI videos via RunComfy CLI with model routing across 7 video model families.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 210 potential issues but all are false positives from documentation content. The 117 'external_commands' findings are markdown code blocks showing runcomfy CLI usage examples. The 61 'hardcoded URL' findings are legitimate RunComfy documentation links. The 14 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings and 15 'system reconnaissance' findings are scanner misinterpretations of model names and comparison tables. The skill is a reference document for an AI agent to invoke runcomfy CLI with proper scoping via the 'allowed-tools' header. No malicious intent found.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill documents runcomfy CLI commands that instruct the AI agent to execute shell commands. The allowed-tools header restricts execution to 'runcomfy *' only. All commands shown use hardcoded model IDs and parameters with no user input injection risk. This is intentional and properly scoped CLI usage, not a vulnerability.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation code blocks in markdown showing command syntax. The skill is designed to instruct execution but is properly scoped via allowed-tools header. No command injection vector exists.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill contains many hardcoded URLs pointing to runcomfy.com, skills.sh, and artificialanalysis.ai. All URLs are legitimate documentation and model-catalog links for the RunComfy platform. No evidence of data exfiltration or malicious destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs verified as legitimate RunComfy platform domains. PTM tracking parameters are standard marketing attribution, not malicious.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Path Reference in Security Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill references ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json in the Security and Privacy section. This documents where the API token is stored as a security best-practice warning. It is not an actual filesystem access instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is clearly a security documentation note warning users about token storage, not an instruction to read or write files. The context explicitly warns 'Never echo the token into a prompt'.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Pattern in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Backtick or code-block shell commands used throughout the markdown file to show runcomfy CLI invocation syntax. These are documentation examples, not executable scripts. The allowed-tools header restricts execution to 'runcomfy *' commands only.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network URL References
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple hardcoded URLs to runcomfy.com and related domains. These are documentation links for model catalogs and CLI documentation. No external callbacks or telemetry endpoints detected.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos Across Multiple Models Via One CLI
content.value_statement: Creating AI videos usually means learning a different tool for each model. This skill gives you a single CLI that routes to every major video generation model including HappyHorse, Kling, Veo, Seedance, and Wan. You can generate videos from text or images, add synchronized audio, and extend existing clips without switching platforms.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using models like HappyHorse 1.0, Kling 3.0, Wan 2-7, and Seedance v2
Animate still images into video clips with motion and audio using image-to-video models
Route requests to the best video model based on user intent, quality needs, speed, or cost
Extend existing Veo clips with motion-consistent continuations via the extend-video endpoint
Generate synchronized audio in-pass with HappyHorse 1.0 without a separate Foley or audio step
Drive lip-sync from a specific voiceover audio file using Wan 2-7 audio_url input
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI (runcomfy) to be installed and authenticated before use
Generation quality depends on the selected model - no single model excels at every video task
Cannot edit or modify existing videos beyond extending them (see the video-edit skill for editing)
Input images and audio must be hosted at URLs that are accessible to the RunComfy API

use_cases.title: Social Media Video Production
use_cases.description: Generate short vertical videos from text or images for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts with in-pass audio and aspect ratio control.
use_cases.target_user: Content creator or social media manager

use_cases.title: Product and Brand Video Prototyping
use_cases.description: Animate product stills with physics-accurate motion, create cinematic ad frames with multi-reference conditioning, and iterate on visual concepts before final production.

use_cases.title: Automated Video Generation Pipeline
use_cases.description: Integrate video generation into automated workflows by scripting runcomfy CLI invocations with batch processing, consistent output directories, and error handling for CI/CD pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video from this description: A red kite tumbles across a windy beach at golden hour, kids chasing it laughing, surf in the background. Use HappyHorse 1.0 with 8 second duration, 16:9 aspect ratio, 1080p resolution.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wanting a simple video from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Video with Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image with HappyHorse 1.0 image-to-video: [image_url]. The subject turns their head slowly toward the camera and smiles. Gentle breeze through hair. Audio: soft wind and ambient music. Use 9:16 aspect ratio, 6 second duration.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with a still image who wants to animate it with synchronized audio

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Cinematic Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cinematic video using Seedance v2 Pro with this prompt: Anamorphic 35mm shot of a vintage car driving down a coastal road at dusk with lens flares. Use these references: [image_url_1] for subject, [image_url_2] for mood, [audio_url] for soundtrack. Duration 10 seconds, 21:9 aspect ratio.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user with multiple reference assets for a polished cinematic result

prompt_templates.title: Audio-Driven Lip-Sync Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a talking video with Wan 2-7 using audio-driven lip-sync. Prompt: Studio portrait of a woman in her 30s speaking confidently to camera, soft window light. Audio file: [voiceover_mp3_url]. Duration 6 seconds. Use audio_url field for lip-sync matching.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with a voiceover recording who wants the on-screen subject to lip-sync to it

output_examples.input: Text description of a beach scene at golden hour with children playing
output_examples.output: An 8-second 1080p video showing a red kite tumbling across a windy beach with children running and laughing, surf audible in the background, warm golden-hour lighting throughout.

output_examples.input: Product still image with instruction to rotate 180 degrees
output_examples.output: A 6-second video of a product bottle slowly rotating 180 degrees on a marble surface with soft daylight, no unwanted background motion, maintaining object permanence throughout the clip.

output_examples.input: Voiceover audio file with prompt describing a speaker addressing the camera
output_examples.output: A 6-second talking-head video where the on-screen subject's mouth movements match the provided voiceover audio track, with soft window lighting and a calm professional framing.

best_practices: Match the model to the user's primary need: HappyHorse for general text-to-video with audio, Veo for physics-accurate motion, Wan for lip-sync, and Seedance for multi-modal cinematic frames.
Write prompts that lead with the subject and one strong action verb instead of stacking adjectives. Describe audio inline for models that support in-pass audio generation.
Use the --output-dir flag to keep generated files organized and set explicit seed values when reproducibility is needed across generations.
anti_patterns: Do not stack multiple unrelated actions in one prompt - the model will average them instead of executing each action clearly. Keep prompts focused on one primary motion.
Do not request in-pass audio from models that do not support it such as Veo and Kling. Only HappyHorse and Seedance generate audio in the same pass.
Do not describe the scene the image already shows in image-to-video prompts. The image provides the scene context; the prompt should only describe the desired motion.

faq.question: What do I need to install before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need the RunComfy CLI installed via npm: npm install -g @runcomfy/cli. Then sign in with runcomfy login or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: Which video model should I use by default?
faq.answer: HappyHorse 1.0 is the recommended default for general text-to-video. It generates synchronized audio in-pass, supports up to 15-second clips, and has strong multi-shot character consistency.

faq.question: Can I generate videos with synchronized audio?
faq.answer: Yes. HappyHorse 1.0 and Seedance v2 generate synchronized audio in the same pass. Describe the audio inline in your prompt using Audio: followed by sound descriptions.

faq.question: How do I animate an existing image into a video?
faq.answer: Use an image-to-video endpoint. Provide the image URL via the image_url field in the input JSON along with a prompt that describes the motion you want. Do not describe what the image already shows.

faq.question: What is the maximum video duration I can generate?
faq.answer: Duration depends on the model. HappyHorse 1.0 supports up to approximately 15 seconds. Veo 3-1 supports 8-second clips which can be extended using the extend-video endpoint.

faq.question: How does the skill choose which model to use?
faq.answer: The skill classifies your request by type (text-to-video, image-to-video, extend) and routes to the best model based on your intent. It considers quality needs, speed, cost, audio requirements, and multi-shot consistency.
